mnemonics: OPEN (Outward, Permeable, Every Neighborhood) spheres are like a circle without its edge, while CLOSED (Containing Limits, Outer Shell Defined) spheres are like a circle with its edge drawn in.
keyPoints: An open sphere S(x, r) is the set of all points y such that the distance d(x, y) < r.
A closed sphere is the set of all points y such that the distance d(x, y) ≤ r.
The key difference is the use of a strict inequality (<) for open spheres versus a non-strict inequality (≤) for closed spheres.
commonMistakes: Assuming a closed sphere is the closure of an open sphere; this is true in familiar spaces like R^n but not in every metric space.
Confusing the concepts with open and closed sets; while related, a sphere being open or closed is a definition of the object itself, not a property derived from a topology.
